text.title: feature-flags Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for feature-flags as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Flag debt: releasing flags that are never removed after full rollout.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each permanent flag multiplies code paths. 10 flags create up to 2^10 theoretical combinations; testing all of them is impossible.

failure-mode.description: Nested flags: checking flag A inside a block already gated by flag B.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Nested flags create an exponential state space. Combinations quickly become untestable and the interaction behavior is unpredictable.

failure-mode.description: Using feature flags for configuration values (timeouts, limits, feature counts).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flags are boolean or variant gates. Configuration values belong in environment variables or config files with typed schemas, not flag systems.

failure-mode.description: Flag debt: ~80% of projects have flags older than six months still in code, raising cyclomatic complexity and obscuring intent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without aggressive cleanup policies and CI enforcement, flags accumulate indefinitely.

failure-mode.description: Cross-service drift: same flag key, different semantics in service A vs service B, leading to user-visible split states.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flags must be evaluated consistently across all services touching a user session. Use a centralized flag service or strict documentation.

failure-mode.description: Missing default in config: undefined flag → is_enabled returns False → feature stays dark in prod indefinitely.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every flag must have an explicit default value recorded in the registry. Config loading must fail loudly if a flag is referenced but not defined.

failure-mode.description: Auth-coupled flags: gating premium features off user.role lookup — when the role lookup fails, paying users get blocked.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Separate flag evaluation from authentication. If auth fails, fall back to the previous state, do not flip all permission flags to OFF.

failure-mode.description: A/B leakage: user opens two tabs and gets two variants; analytics treats it as one user with conflicting events.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bucketing is deterministic per user, but a single user in two tabs should get the same variant. Store bucket assignment server-side or enforce session-level consistency.

failure-mode.description: Provider outage = your outage if no fail-safe default per call (LaunchDarkly / Statsig down → all flags evaluate to OFF, breaking premium customers).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always have a local cache and fallback default. Evaluate flags against stale cached data rather than failing open or closed.

failure-mode.description: PII leakage: passing entire user objects to the flag service is common; it's a hidden compliance risk (GDPR, HIPAA).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Only pass user ID and a small set of targeting attributes (plan, region, cohort). Never pass email, name, or raw user objects.

failure-mode.description: Test coverage usually stops at the ON branch; OFF branch rots and causes ghost bugs when the flag is removed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Treat both branches as equally important. Enforce test coverage gates that require both ON and OFF tests.

failure-mode.description: Agents add if flag_enabled("foo") everywhere instead of at one decision point — multiplies branches and makes cleanup hard.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Concentrate flag checks at decision boundaries. Use a decorator or context manager, not scattered conditionals.

failure-mode.description: LLMs forget the OFF-branch tests. Force the gate at PR-review time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Treat untested OFF branches as coverage gaps, not acceptable. Require both _on_test.py and _off_test.py.

failure-mode.description: When generating an eval client, agents often hardcode default=True so tests pass locally — leaks features to prod.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always set default=False unless the flag is explicitly a kill-switch. Fail loudly if no default is defined.

failure-mode.description: Removal asymmetry: agents may delete the if check but leave the now-dead OFF branch (or vice versa). Use piranha or strict review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pair the removal with both-branch cleanup or use automated tools like piranha that handle the full transformation.

failure-mode.description: Targeting attribute leak: agents pass entire user objects to the flag service. Whitelist attributes (id + plan + region) at the SDK boundary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Only pass the minimum required attributes. Create a DTO or whitelist that prevents full object serialization to the flag service.

failure-mode.description: Flag-name drift: agents create new_checkout_v2 then new_checkout_v3 instead of editing the existing flag. Lock the registry; agents must rename, not duplicate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce a naming convention and require human approval for new flag definitions. Prevent agent-driven proliferation of similar flags.

failure-mode.description: Incident response: agents asked to disable feature X sometimes flip the wrong flag. Require explicit flag key + dry-run + diff before applying in prod.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always ask for the exact flag key and show the dry-run (which flags will flip and their current values) before executing.

failure-mode.description: Rollback ambiguity: when an agent flips a flag back, it may not record the reason in the audit log — make the reason field mandatory on every flag change.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every flag change (enable, disable, rollout percentage) must have a reason recorded. Use this for post-incident reviews.
